meta.description: Spike detection and probability model for Deriv Boom and Crash indices.

spikeBasics.definition: A spike is a sudden large one-directional candle unique to Boom (spikes UP) and Crash (spikes DOWN) indices.
spikeBasics.keyTruth: Averages are statistical, NOT schedules. A spike can come early or very late. Never trade on tick-count alone.

detectionAlgorithm.spikeCandle: A candle is a spike if its range > 3 x ATR(14) AND it moves in the spike direction of the index.
steps: 2. Track ticks/candles elapsed since last detected spike.
3. Compute drift strength: consecutive counter-drift candles (down-drift on Boom, up-drift on Crash).
4. Compute proximity to key demand (Boom) or supply (Crash) zones on M5/M15.
5. Score spike probability using the factors below.

probabilityFactors.factor: price at strong HTF demand zone (Boom) / supply zone (Crash)
RSI(14) M1 oversold on Boom / overbought on Crash
price at round number / psych level

thresholds.low: 0-39: no spike edge, avoid spike trades
thresholds.high: 65-84: spike zone active, valid for spike strategies

boomSpikeCatching.goal: Catch upward spikes on Boom indices.
rules: Enter buy at strong M5/M15 demand zone during down-drift.
SL below the demand zone (structure-based, ~1.5 x ATR).
TP: spike usually covers many drift candles; take profit immediately after spike prints or use trailing stop.
Exit if 2x average tick count passes with no spike and structure breaks down.

crashSpikeCatching.goal: Catch downward spikes on Crash indices.
rules: Enter sell at strong M5/M15 supply zone during up-drift.
TP immediately after spike prints or trail.
On Boom: the drift is DOWN. Sell the drift only right AFTER a spike (fresh cycle, spike unlikely soon).
On Crash: the drift is UP. Buy the drift only right after a spike.
Hard exit before tick count approaches the average — never hold a counter-spike position into an overdue zone.
Use M1 EMA9/21 alignment with the drift for entries.
Small targets, strict stops: one spike can erase many drift wins.

postSpikeScalp.goal: Trade continuation right after a spike.
rules: After a Boom spike, price typically resumes down-drift: scalp sells on M1 rejection patterns.
After a Crash spike, price typically resumes up-drift: scalp buys.
Only in the first portion of a new cycle.

riskWarnings: Never SELL Boom or BUY Crash near an overdue spike window — a single spike can blow the stop massively.
Spread and slippage widen around spikes; account for it in SL placement.
Spike catching without a zone confluence is gambling; the tick average alone is not an edge.

dataNeeded.ticks: Live tick stream from Deriv WebSocket API (see realtime-data.json).
dataNeeded.candles: M1, M5, M15 OHLC for zone and ATR calculations.
dataNeeded.state: Persist lastSpikeTimestamp, ticksSinceSpike, and rolling ATR per symbol.
